tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Navigate to Security group on the left panel and click on Create Security group.
Fill in the below details under Basic details Name: Enter ElastiCache-SG Description: Enter Security group for ElastiCache VPC: Select Default VPC
Fill in the below details under Basic details
Name: Enter ElastiCache-SG
Description: Enter Security group for ElastiCache
VPC: Select Default VPC

In the Inbound rules, We will add the incoming traffic details: To add SSH, Click on: Add rule Choose Type: SSH Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0 For HTTP, Click on: Add rule Choose Type: HTTP Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0 For Redis, Click on: Add rule Choose Type: Custom TCP Port range: Enter 6379 Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0 After that, click on Create security group
In the Inbound rules, We will add the incoming traffic details:
To add SSH, Click on: Add rule Choose Type: SSH Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0
To add SSH,
Click on: Add rule
Choose Type: SSH
Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0
For HTTP, Click on: Add rule Choose Type: HTTP Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0
For HTTP,
Click on: Add rule
Choose Type: HTTP
Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0
For Redis, Click on: Add rule Choose Type: Custom TCP Port range: Enter 6379 Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0 After that, click on Create security group
For Redis,
Click on: Add rule
Choose Type: Custom TCP
Port range: Enter 6379
Source: Select Anywhere-IPv4, enter 0.0.0.0/0
After that, click on Create security group
Security group is now created. We will use that for EC2 and Redis cluster.
Navigate to ElastiCache by clicking on the Services menu in the top. Then click on Redis OSS caches under Resources and select Create cache button.

We will now create a Redis Cluster, Select Continue with Redis OSS Engine : Select Redis OSSDeployment option : Select Node-based cluster Creation method : Select Cluster cache Cluster Mode : Select Enabled
We will now create a Redis Cluster, Select Continue with Redis OSS
Task 3 : Create a Redis Cluster
Engine : Select Redis OSS
Deployment option : Select Node-based cluster
Creation method : Select Cluster cache
Cluster Mode : Select Enabled
Task 3 : Create a Redis Cluster
Cluster info: Name: Enter MyRedisCluster Description: Enter Redis Cluster for WhizProject
Name: Enter MyRedisCluster
Description: Enter Redis Cluster for WhizProject
Location: Location : Choose AWS Cloud Check the Multi AZ Enabled checkbox
Location:
Location : Choose AWS Cloud
Check the Multi AZ Enabled checkbox
Task 3 : Create a Redis Cluster
In Cluster settings section, fill the details as follows:
Engine version: Leave it as default
Port: 6379 (default)
Parameter group: Leave it as default
Node type: cache.t3.micro (0.5 GiB)
Number of Shards: 1
Replicas per Shard: 1
Task 3 : Create a Redis Cluster
Subnet group settings: Choose Create new subnet group Name: Enter elasticache-subnet-group Description: Enter Subnet group for ElastiCache VPC ID: Default VPC Click on Next button.
Subnet group settings: Choose Create new subnet group
Name: Enter elasticache-subnet-group
Description: Enter Subnet group for ElastiCache
VPC ID: Default VPC
Click on Next button.
For Advanced Settings
In Selected security groups, click on Manage button.
Select ElastiCache-SG, and click on the Choose button.
In the Backup section: Enable automatic backups: Uncheck Leave other options as default Click on Next button.
In the Backup section:
Enable automatic backups: Uncheck
Leave other options as default
Click on Next button.
Finally, Click on the Create button.
It will take up to 10 minutes for this cluster to go from creating state to available state.
Meanwhile, let's create an EC2 instance, which will be used for accessing the Redis cluster.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Navigate to Instances on the left panel and click on Launch Instance.
Enter Name as MyEC2Server
Choose an Amazon Machine Image (AMI): Select Amazon Linux 2023 AMI in the drop-down.
Choose architecture as 64-bit(x86)
Task 4 : Launching an EC2 Instance
Choose an Instance Type: Select t2.micro.
Task 4 : Launching an EC2 Instance
Key Pair: Select Create a new key pair
Key Pair name : Enter MyWhizkey
Key pair type: Choose RSA
Private key file format: Choose .pem
Click on Create Key Pair button.
Under Network Settings, choose Select existing security group.
Select ElastiCache-SG security group created in the Task 2
Task 4 : Launching an EC2 Instance
On the right side under the Summary section,
Number of Instances: 1
Leave the rest of the things as default.
Launch Status: Your instance is now launching, Click on the instance ID and wait for complete initialization of instance till status change to running.
To work with the ElastiCache cluster, we need the endpoint.
Navigate to ElastiCache by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on Elasticache in the Database section
On the home page, click on Redis Clusters in the left navigation panel.
If the status of the ElastiCache cluster is available.
Task 5 : Copy the ElastiCache Endpoint
Click on the cluster name and Copy the Configuration Endpoint, and save it in the notepad, we'll use it in the later steps.
Task 5 : Copy the ElastiCache Endpoint

Make sure to remove the port number from the end. After removing the port number it will be:
Example: myrediscluster.n62wo6.clustercfg.use1.cache.amazonaws.com
Please follow the steps in SSH into EC2 Instance.

tasks.title: Task 7: Install the required package and connect to the ElastiCache cluster

steps.text: Run the below code in the terminal.
Copy the below code, paste the script and save with CTRL+X, enter Y and then click the Enter button.
Run the below commands to execute the script.
Go to the directory

Now, connect to your Redis clusterNote: Replace the Endpoint with the copied one. Make sure to paste the endpoint without colon(:) and port numberExample: src/redis-cli -c --tls -h clustercfg.myrediscluster.2klkvn.use1.cache.amazonaws.com -p 6379
Task 7: Install the required package and connect to the ElastiCache cluster

tasks.title: Task 8 : Test the ElastiCache cluster by running sample commands

steps.text: Enter the first command as.
This command will set the value of a as "hello", a string value, with no expiry
Print the value of a
This command will set the value of b as "Whiz", a string value, with 10-second expiry
If you execute this command with-in 10 seconds, you will be able to see the value of b.
Re-enter this command after 10 seconds, to see the value of b is visible or not.
Once completed, enter the below command to close the connection with the Redis cluster.

Creating a Redis cluster using Amazon ElastiCache allows you to set up a highly available and scalable Redis deployment in the AWS cloud. Amazon ElastiCache is a managed service that simplifies the deployment, management, and monitoring of Redis clusters, providing high performance, low-latency data storage for various applications.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the right-side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 9: Validation of the lab
You have successfully created a Redis cluster with ElastiCache.
You have successfully logged into the EC2 instance by SSH.
You have successfully tested the Redis cluster.
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
